tasks.title: Disable auto-commit and implement explicit transactions in DatabaseConfig
tasks.description: Modify database configuration to disable auto-commit and enable explicit transaction management for atomic operations.
tasks.details: Update DatabaseConfig.kt to set isAutoCommit = false in HikariConfig. Use Kotlin coroutines withContext(Dispatchers.IO) for database operations. Implement a suspend extension function transactionSuspend on DSLContext that wraps operations in a transaction block using jOOQ's transaction method.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that save(), update(), and deleteById() repository methods execute within a single transaction by simulating failures between operations and ensuring no partial commits occur. Use integration tests with rollback verification.

tasks.title: Refactor repository methods to use transactionSuspend for atomic operations
tasks.description: Update repository methods save(), update(), and deleteById() to wrap all database calls within transactionSuspend to ensure atomicity.
tasks.details: In repository implementations like CityRepositoryImpl and RestroomRepositoryImpl, wrap all related DB calls (e.g., insert and select) inside ctx.transactionSuspend { txCtx -> ... } to ensure they execute in one transaction. Remove any auto-commit reliance.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit and integration tests to confirm that partial failures do not leave inconsistent data. Test rollback behavior on exceptions within transactionSuspend blocks.

tasks.title: Create extension helper for coordinate field projections to eliminate duplication
tasks.description: Implement an extension function or property to provide coordinate fields projection to avoid repeated code in repositories.
tasks.details: Create an extension function like selectWithCoordinates() on relevant table references (e.g., RESTROOMS) returning a list of fields including latAlias() and lonAlias(). Replace duplicated coordinate field declarations in repository methods with this helper.
tasks.testStrategy: Code review to ensure all coordinate field usages are replaced. Unit tests for repository methods to verify correct fields are selected.

tasks.title: Add fetchCount parameter to executePaginated and optimize count queries
tasks.description: Modify executePaginated to accept a fetchCount boolean parameter to optionally skip COUNT queries for performance optimization.
tasks.details: Update QueryExecutor.kt to propagate fetchCount parameter. For endpoints like /nearest, call executePaginated with fetchCount=false to avoid expensive COUNT(*) queries. Consider approximate counts or caching for large tables in future iterations.
tasks.testStrategy: Benchmark response times with fetchCount true vs false on large datasets. Verify pagination behavior remains correct when count is skipped.

tasks.title: Implement in-memory caching for countries and cities using Caffeine
tasks.description: Add caching layer for frequently requested read-only data like countries and cities to reduce database load.
tasks.details: Add Caffeine dependency version 3.1.8. Create CacheConfig.kt to configure caches with maximumSize and expireAfterWrite TTL. Inject caches into CountryService and CityService. Use cache.get(key) with loader lambda for reads. Invalidate cache on writes.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for cache hit/miss scenarios. Integration tests to verify cache invalidation after create/update operations.

tasks.title: Refactor long repository methods by extracting query building logic
tasks.description: Improve code readability by extracting complex query construction into smaller private methods in repositories.
tasks.details: In RestroomRepositoryImpl and CityRepositoryImpl, create private methods like buildInsertQuery() and buildReturningFields() that return InsertSetStep and list of fields respectively. Use these methods in save() and update() to reduce method length and duplication.
tasks.testStrategy: Code review for method length reduction. Unit tests to verify extracted methods produce correct queries and results.

tasks.title: Extract magic numbers and strings into centralized constants object
tasks.description: Centralize all magic numbers and strings used in validation, pagination, and defaults into a Constants.kt object for maintainability.
tasks.details: Create ApiConstants object in config package with constants like DEFAULT_PAGE_SIZE=10, MAX_PAGE_SIZE=100, MAX_NAME_LENGTH=255, etc. Replace all hardcoded literals in codebase with references to these constants.
tasks.testStrategy: Code inspection to ensure no magic numbers remain. Regression tests to confirm behavior unchanged.

tasks.title: Standardize error messages and implement i18n-ready error constants
tasks.description: Centralize all error messages in a resource file or constants object and standardize language to English for API consistency and future i18n support.
tasks.details:
Create ErrorMessages object with formatted string constants for common errors. Replace inline error strings in exceptions and logs with references to ErrorMessages. Consider integration with i18n libraries for future multi-language support.
<info added on 2025-11-30T10:29:34.826Z>
Task deferred for later implementation. Per PROGRESS_TRACKING.md, error messages will remain in Russian. This task has low priority and will be completed in a future sprint.
</info added on 2025-11-30T10:29:34.826Z>

Recent validation logic has been added to DatabaseConfig.kt in the init block, covering required fields (host, name, user, password), port range (1-65535), positive values for maxPoolSize, connectionTimeout, idleTimeout, maxLifetime, and minIdle constraints (>=0 and <= maxPoolSize, idleTimeout <= maxLifetime). DatabaseConfigTest.kt includes tests for all boundary cases. Error messages from this validation should also be centralized and standardized as part of this task, ensuring consistency with future API error handling standards (e.g., RFC 7807 or RFC 9457 for structured error responses).
tasks.testStrategy: Verify all error messages are replaced. Test error responses for correct message formatting. Ensure validation error messages from DatabaseConfig.kt are included and follow the standardized format.

tasks.title: Expand DTO validation to cover all fields with length and structure checks
tasks.description: Enhance validation logic for DTOs to include all relevant fields with proper length limits and JSON structure validation.
tasks.details: In RestroomValidators.kt, add validation rules for name, description, phones, workTime, and amenities fields. Use maxLength constraints from ApiConstants. Implement validateJsonObject for JSON structure validation of phones and workTime fields.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for validation rules with valid and invalid inputs. Test rejection of overly long strings and malformed JSON.

tasks.title: Standardize validation to occur only in controllers, remove service-level validation
tasks.description: Refactor validation logic to be performed exclusively in controllers, ensuring services receive already validated data.
tasks.details: Remove validateOrThrow calls from service methods like CityService.createCity(). Keep validateAndThen in controllers. Services should only perform business logic validation and throw domain exceptions if needed.
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests to verify invalid data is rejected at controller level. Confirm services do not perform redundant validation.

tasks.title: Replace reflection-based validation error path extraction with official konform API
tasks.description: Improve reliability and performance of validation error path extraction by using konform's official API instead of reflection and string parsing.
tasks.details: Update ValidationExtension.kt to use konform's API for error path retrieval. Implement caching if necessary to optimize repeated calls. Remove fragile string parsing logic.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests for error path extraction correctness. Performance tests comparing old and new implementations.

tasks.title: Enhance PSQLException handling to cover multiple SQL error states
tasks.description: Expand database error handling to differentiate unique violations, foreign key violations, check constraints, not-null violations, and others with specific HTTP responses.
tasks.details:
In ErrorHandlingPlugin.kt, extend exception handler for PSQLException to switch on sqlState codes like 23505, 23503, 23514, 23502, 42P01. Map each to appropriate HTTP status and error message. Log errors with context.
<info added on 2025-11-30T10:22:21.251Z>
PSQLException handling for error codes 23505, 23503, 23502, 23514, and 42P01 has already been implemented with appropriate HTTP status mappings. The remaining work focuses on two areas: (1) structured logging with context information (request path, HTTP method, status code, query parameters) which is being handled as a separate task 14, and (2) updating integration tests to verify correct HTTP responses and error messages for various database error scenarios including unique constraint violations, foreign key violations, not-null violations, check constraint violations, and table not found errors.
</info added on 2025-11-30T10:22:21.251Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Integration tests simulating various SQL exceptions and verifying correct HTTP responses and messages.

subtasks.title: Update integration tests for PSQLException handling
subtasks.description: Add integration tests to verify correct HTTP responses and error messages for various database error scenarios (23505, 23503, 23502, 23514, 42P01)

tasks.title: Replace error() calls with specific repository exceptions
tasks.description: Create custom exception classes for repository errors and replace generic error() calls with throwing these exceptions for better error classification.
tasks.details: Define RepositoryException and EntityNotFoundException classes. Replace error() calls in repositories with throw RepositoryException or EntityNotFoundException with descriptive messages. Update error handling to recognize these exceptions.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests to verify exceptions are thrown as expected. Logging tests to confirm exception types are logged correctly.

subtasks.title: Update tests for new repository exceptions
subtasks.description: Update existing tests to verify that RepositoryException and EntityNotFoundException are thrown correctly and handled properly

tasks.title: Add structured logging with context for error handling
tasks.description: Improve error logs by including structured context such as request path, method, status, and query parameters for better diagnostics.
tasks.details: Enhance ErrorHandlingPlugin.kt to log exceptions with structured data maps including path, HTTP method, status code, and query parameters. Use logger.warn or logger.error accordingly.
tasks.testStrategy: Review logs during error scenarios to confirm presence of structured context. Automated tests to verify logging calls.

tasks.title: Reorganize model package to separate DTOs and entities
tasks.description: Refactor project package structure to clearly separate DTOs and entity classes for better maintainability and clarity.
tasks.details: Create model/dto and model/entity subpackages. Move CityCreateDto, CityResponseDto, etc. to dto/, and CountryEntity, CityEntity to entity/. Update imports and references accordingly.
tasks.testStrategy: Build and run tests to ensure no import errors. Code review for package structure consistency.

tasks.title: Split common package into api, mapper, and query subpackages
tasks.description: Refactor common package to reduce overload by dividing into more focused packages like api.errors, mapper, and query.builder.
tasks.details: Create new packages api.errors, mapper, query.builder. Move relevant classes and files accordingly. Update project references and imports.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify project builds and tests pass after refactoring. Code review for package usage.

tasks.title: Group utility functions in util package by domain
tasks.description: Organize utility classes into domain-specific subpackages within util for better modularity and discoverability.
tasks.details: Create util/http, util/geo, util/db, util/serialization, util/config subpackages. Move ApplicationCallExtensions.kt to http/, GeoDsl.kt to geo/, RepositoryExtensions.kt to db/, Serializers.kt to serialization/, EnvironmentExtensions.kt to config/.
tasks.testStrategy: Build and run tests to confirm no breakage. Code review for consistent usage.

tasks.title: Add comprehensive unit tests for QueryBuilder and QueryExecutor
tasks.description: The task to add unit tests for QueryExecutor has been skipped per user request. Existing tests on services and controllers already cover QueryExecutor functionality. The task is marked as completed.
tasks.details: Tests for QueryExecutor were removed because service and controller tests sufficiently cover its functionality. Focus remains on QueryBuilder tests if needed separately. All previous fixes and improvements to QueryExecutorTest compilation and runtime issues are preserved in completed subtasks.
tasks.testStrategy: No additional unit tests for QueryExecutor will be added. Existing service and controller tests provide coverage. The task is considered complete based on user direction.

subtasks.title: Implement parameterized tests for edge cases
subtasks.description: Create parameterized tests to cover edge cases for all filter operators and query execution scenarios.
subtasks.details: This subtask is no longer applicable as tests for QueryExecutor have been removed per user request. Focus remains on existing service and controller tests for coverage.
subtasks.testStrategy: No tests implemented due to task skip.

subtasks.title: Generate coverage reports
subtasks.description: Run tests with coverage reports to ensure critical paths are tested.
subtasks.details: This subtask is no longer applicable as the testing scope for QueryExecutor has been removed. Coverage reports should be reviewed on service and controller tests instead.
subtasks.testStrategy: No coverage reports generated for QueryExecutor tests due to task skip.

subtasks.title: Fix compilation errors in QueryExecutorTest.kt
subtasks.description: Add imports for extension functions, correct mock chains for JOOQ types, mock where() on SelectWhereStep, and fix map() call types.
subtasks.details: Ensure all required imports for extension functions are present. Correct the mock setup for JOOQ types to ensure proper type chaining. Mock the where() method on SelectWhereStep for use in applyAdditionalConditions. Fix the types used in map() calls to match expected return types.
subtasks.testStrategy: Verify that tests compile without errors.

subtasks.title: Fix runtime failures in QueryExecutorTest
subtasks.description: Improve mock logic for extension functions and private methods to ensure tests pass at runtime.
subtasks.details:
Refactor mocks to properly handle extension functions and private methods. Ensure that all mocked objects behave as expected during test execution.
<info added on 2025-11-30T11:48:17.704Z>
Проверены и исправлены моки для всех цепочек вызовов: ensure that where(), orderBy(), and(), and map() are properly mocked with correct return types and behaviors. Убедились, что моки для extension функций applyFilters и applySorting корректно делегируют вызовы реальным методам jOOQ. Все тесты запущены — оставшиеся runtime ошибки исправлены путем настройки правильных возвращаемых значений и последовательности вызовов на моках.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for mappers with mocked jOOQ Records
tasks.description: Create unit tests for RestroomMapper, CityMapper, and CountryMapper to verify correct mapping from jOOQ Records to DTOs.
tasks.details: Use mocking frameworks like MockK to mock Record objects with expected field values. Test mapFromRecord methods to ensure all fields are correctly mapped.
tasks.testStrategy: Unit tests asserting mapped DTO fields equal expected values from mocked records.

tasks.title: Add unit tests for utility functions and edge cases
tasks.description: Implement unit tests for utility classes like GeoDsl, ApplicationCallExtensions, and RepositoryExtensions including edge cases and boundary conditions.
tasks.details: Write tests for GeoDsl functions like pointExpr and distanceGeographyTo. Mock ApplicationCall to test toPaginationRequest parsing. Add parameterized tests for edge cases such as empty lists, nulls, and invalid values.
tasks.testStrategy: Use parameterized and boundary tests to cover edge cases. Verify expected exceptions or results.

tasks.title: Implement health check endpoints with database connectivity verification
tasks.description: Create health check endpoints (/health, /health/ready, /health/live) that include database connectivity status, implemented in HealthController.kt with routing configured in RoutingConfig.kt, and provide comprehensive unit tests.
tasks.details:
1. Create HealthController.kt to define three endpoints: /health, /health/ready, and /health/live. Each endpoint should return appropriate HTTP status and JSON indicating service health.

2. Implement a database connectivity check within the health endpoints by executing a lightweight query or ping against the database using the existing database configuration (ensuring it uses explicit transactions as per Task 1).

3. Integrate these endpoints into the application's routing by adding routes in RoutingConfig.kt, ensuring they are accessible and follow REST conventions.

4. Structure the health responses to comply with common microservice health check standards, distinguishing readiness and liveness checks (e.g., readiness includes database connectivity, liveness confirms service is running).

5. Write unit tests for HealthController to verify each endpoint returns correct status codes and payloads under normal and simulated failure conditions (e.g., database down).

6. Follow Kotlin coroutine best practices for asynchronous database calls and HTTP handling.

7. Ensure logging and error handling are consistent with existing project standards.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Unit test each health endpoint to assert HTTP 200 OK with expected JSON when the database is reachable.

2. Simulate database connectivity failure and verify endpoints return appropriate failure status (e.g., HTTP 503 Service Unavailable) and error details.

3. Test routing configuration to confirm endpoints are correctly registered and accessible.

4. Perform integration tests to confirm health endpoints reflect real database connectivity status.

5. Code review to ensure adherence to project coding standards and proper use of coroutines and transactions.

tasks.title: Implement Local-First Task Management for Cursor Agent
tasks.description: Enable Cursor Agent to perform all read operations directly from the local tasks.json file and cache task context in memory during the session, minimizing API calls to Task Master.
tasks.details: Implement file system reading of `.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json` for fetching task lists, filtering by status, priority, and dependencies. Parse the `details` field of tasks and subtasks for context. Cache this context in memory for session duration to avoid repeated file reads. Restrict API calls to only write operations such as `update_subtask`, `add_task`, and `expand_task`. Ensure all implementation details are stored within the `details` field of tasks and subtasks. All rule files and documentation have been consolidated and optimized: duplicate and redundant files have been removed, integration rules merged into a single `taskmaster_integration.mdc`, and all references updated to point to the correct files. The context size has been significantly reduced while preserving all functionality.
tasks.testStrategy: Validate that all read operations do not trigger API calls by monitoring network requests. Test correct parsing and filtering of tasks from the local JSON file. Verify in-memory cache updates and consistency during session. Confirm that write operations still use API calls as expected. Ensure that the consolidated rule and documentation files are correctly referenced and that no legacy files are accessed.

tasks.title: Develop Automated Workflow Integration from User Task to Subtasks
tasks.description: Automate the entire workflow from receiving a new user task through generating and working on subtasks without explicit user mention of Task Master.
tasks.details: Implement a pipeline that upon receiving a new user task: 1) creates a structured task description, 2) formats it into a PRD, 3) calls `parse_prd` API to generate subtasks, 4) retrieves generated subtasks, and 5) proceeds to work strictly on these subtasks. Ensure this workflow triggers automatically and integrates seamlessly with Cursor Agent and Task Master.
tasks.testStrategy: Test with various new user tasks to confirm automatic creation of PRD and subtasks. Verify that subtasks are correctly generated and that the agent works only on these subtasks. Confirm no manual intervention or explicit Task Master mention is required.

tasks.title: Implement Context Preservation and Structured Logging
tasks.description: Ensure all implementation details and progress are logged in the `details` field using a structured format to enable full context preservation and recovery.
tasks.details: Use the `update_subtask` API to log progress and implementation details in a structured format including Plan, Files, Changes, Issues, Progress, and Next steps. Implement saving of research outputs via `research --save-file`. Enforce a maximum length of 50,000 characters for the `details` field. Structure logs to facilitate easy parsing and recovery.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that all updates to subtasks include structured logs in the `details` field. Test that research files are saved correctly. Confirm that the length limit is respected and logs remain parseable. Simulate recovery scenarios to ensure context completeness.

tasks.title: Create Reusable Configuration Templates for Task Master and Cursor Agent
tasks.description: Develop configuration templates for Task Master and Cursor Agent to enable easy reuse and automatic application for new projects.
tasks.details:
Templates have been created and configurations updated for optimal reuse and automatic workflow:

- `.taskmaster/templates/config-template.json` - Task Master config with optimal settings
- `.taskmaster/templates/agent-rules-template.mdc` - Agent integration rules
- `.taskmaster/templates/workflow-template.mdc` - Automatic workflow template

Configuration files updated:
- `.taskmaster/config.json` - Added agentOptimization section
- `.cursor/rules/taskmaster_integration.mdc` - Main integration rules
- `.cursor/rules/taskmaster/auto_workflow.mdc` - Automatic workflow rules
- `.cursor/rules/cursor_agent_taskmaster.mdc` - Agent-specific rules
- `.cursor/rules/tutochka.mdc` - Added Task Master integration section

All templates are ready for reuse in new projects. Configuration is optimized for local-first strategy and automatic workflow.
tasks.testStrategy: Test template files for correctness and completeness. Validate automatic application of templates on new project setup. Confirm that Cursor Agent and Task Master operate with these configurations and that model settings are optimal.

tasks.title: Implement Fallback Recovery Mechanism Using Local Files
tasks.description: Ensure full recovery of task context and state from local files without any API calls in case of failures or restarts.
tasks.details: Develop recovery logic that reads `.taskmaster/tasks/tasks.json` and other local files to reconstruct the entire task and subtask state, including statuses, priorities, dependencies, and detailed logs. Confirm that this recovery works independently of any API calls, providing a complete picture of the current state for Cursor Agent and Task Master.
tasks.testStrategy: Simulate failure and restart scenarios. Verify that recovery from local files restores full context and state accurately. Confirm no API calls are made during recovery. Test consistency of recovered data with pre-failure state.

tasks.title: Optimize API Usage with Batched Write Operations
tasks.description: Reduce the number of API calls by batching updates and write operations to Task Master AI.
tasks.details: Implement batching logic for write operations such as `update_subtask`, `add_task`, and `expand_task`. Accumulate multiple updates in memory and send them in a single API call to minimize overhead and resource consumption. Ensure batching respects session context and does not delay critical updates excessively.
tasks.testStrategy: Measure API call frequency before and after batching implementation. Confirm that batched calls correctly update Task Master state without data loss. Test system responsiveness and consistency under various workloads.

tasks.title: Integrate Economical AI Models for Main and Fallback Operations
tasks.description: Configure and integrate cost-effective AI models (sonar for main/research, gemini-2.0-flash for fallback) to optimize resource usage.
tasks.details: Set up model selection logic to use the sonar model for main and research tasks and gemini-2.0-flash as a fallback model. Ensure seamless switching between models based on availability and task type. Update configuration templates to reflect these model preferences.
tasks.testStrategy: Test model selection under normal and fallback conditions. Validate that tasks use the correct models as per configuration. Monitor resource usage and cost metrics to confirm optimization.

tasks.title: Implement Filtering and Status Checking for Local Task Operations
tasks.description: Enable efficient local filtering of tasks by status, priority, and dependencies, and implement subtask status checks without API calls.
tasks.details: Develop functions to filter tasks read from `tasks.json` based on status, priority, and dependencies. Implement logic to check and update subtask statuses locally by parsing the `details` field. Ensure these operations are performant and accurate to support local-first management and recovery.
tasks.testStrategy: Test filtering accuracy with various task datasets. Validate subtask status detection and updates locally. Confirm no API calls occur during these operations. Benchmark performance for large task sets.

tasks.title: Проверка структуры файлов и директорий
tasks.description: Проверить корректность структуры файлов в .cursor/rules/ и .taskmaster/
tasks.details: Обойти все директории и убедиться, что все требуемые файлы присутствуют и имеют правильную структуру. Проверить наличие TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc, taskmaster_integration.mdc, tutochka.mdc, dev_workflow.mdc, README.md и шаблонов в templates/. Убедиться, что нет лишних или устаревших файлов.
tasks.testStrategy: Сравнить структуру с эталонной схемой, проверить наличие всех файлов, убедиться в отсутствии лишних файлов.

tasks.title: Валидация ссылок между файлами правил

Пройти по всем ссылкам в файлах .mdc и README.md, убедиться, что они ведут на существующие файлы и директории. Проверить корректность путей и форматов ссылок. Проверка ссылок завершена, исправлены битые ссылки:

Все ссылки теперь указывают на существующие файлы.

tasks.title: Проверка отсутствия дублирующихся правил

tasks.details:
Сравнение содержимого всех файлов правил завершено. Дубликатов не обнаружено. Каждый файл имеет свою уникальную роль:
- TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc содержит уникальное описание автоматического workflow
- taskmaster_integration.mdc содержит уникальные интеграционные правила (local-first, API optimization)
- tutochka.mdc содержит правила проекта + ссылку на Task Master интеграцию
Ссылки между файлами корректны и не дублируют содержимое.
tasks.testStrategy: Сравнение содержимого файлов, поиск дубликатов с помощью скрипта, ручная проверка. Подтверждено отсутствие дубликатов и корректность ссылок между файлами.

tasks.title: Валидация автоматического workflow
tasks.description: Проверить, что автоматический workflow описан корректно и работает для всех типов запросов
tasks.details: Протестирован автоматический workflow на различных типах запросов, подтверждена корректность срабатывания и точность описания всех шагов. Все шаги workflow описаны четко в TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc и taskmaster_integration.mdc: Create PRD → parse_prd → Read tasks.json → Work on subtasks → Log progress. Workflow работает автоматически: создан PRD, распарсен, получены задачи 30-35, работа идет по плану. Pattern recognition описан для всех типов запросов, все правила соблюдаются автоматически.
tasks.testStrategy: Тестирование workflow на разных типах запросов, проверка логов и результатов, демонстрация автоматического выполнения всех шагов.

subtasks.title: Проверить описание workflow в документации
subtasks.description: Убедиться, что workflow описан четко в TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc и taskmaster_integration.mdc
subtasks.details: Описание workflow проверено и соответствует реальному процессу.

subtasks.description: Протестировать workflow на различных типах запросов, убедиться в корректности срабатывания
subtasks.details: Workflow протестирован на всех типах запросов, все шаги выполняются автоматически.

tasks.title: Проверка сохранения и восстановления контекста
tasks.description: Убедиться, что контекст сохраняется в поле details задач и восстанавливается из локальных файлов
tasks.details: Проверено, что при старте задач контекст сохраняется в поле details, и при восстановлении он корректно загружается из локальных файлов. Все детали реализации логируются через update_task/update_subtask. Контекст хранится локально в tasks.json, восстановление работает через чтение tasks.json (без API вызовов). Контекст минимален, но достаточен (оптимизированные правила, без избыточности). Все важные функции сохранены после оптимизации. Система готова к использованию.
tasks.testStrategy: Тестирование сохранения и восстановления контекста, проверка логов и результатов. Подтверждено, что контекст сохраняется и восстанавливается корректно, логирование деталей реализации работает, контекст оптимизирован и достаточен для работы.

tasks.title: Add Git Commit Rule After Task Completion
tasks.description: Implement a workflow rule that triggers after a task status is set to 'done' to initiate a git commit.
tasks.details:
Modify `.cursor/rules/TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc` to add a new step after `set_task_status`. This step should check if the task status is 'done' and, if so, proceed to execute the git commit logic. Ensure the rule is non-blocking and gracefully handles errors. The implementation has been completed as follows:
- Step 5 added in TASKMASTER_AUTO_WORKFLOW.mdc for automatic git commit after set_task_status
- New file .cursor/rules/taskmaster/git_commit.mdc created with detailed rules
- Section "Git Commit After Task Completion" added in workflow
- Rule is non-blocking - workflow continues even if git is unavailable
- Commit message template is described in the rules
tasks.testStrategy: Unit test the workflow rule by simulating task completion and verifying that the commit step is triggered only for 'done' tasks. Confirm that workflow continues even if git is unavailable. Test should also validate that the commit message template is correctly applied and that the workflow behaves as expected in edge cases (e.g., no git repo, no changes to commit).

tasks.title: Collect Changed Files and Task Data
tasks.description: Gather detailed information about changed files and task details for generating the commit message, using a structured and documented approach.
tasks.details:
Implement a function or script that performs the following five-step process:
1. Verify if the current directory is a git repository using appropriate git commands.
2. Collect the list of changed files using `git status --porcelain` with detailed parsing to identify file states.
3. Retrieve task data (ID, title, description) locally from `tasks.json` without relying on external APIs.
4. Implement a function `generateCommitMessage` that accepts parameters for changed files and task data to produce a structured commit message.
5. Document all steps, commands, and function usage comprehensively in `git_commit.mdc` for maintainability and clarity.

Include example git commands for repository verification and file collection as part of the implementation documentation.
<info added on 2025-11-30T17:18:42.913Z>
Работа над задачей 37 завершена. В документацию `git_commit.mdc` добавлена детальная информация по всем пяти шагам процесса генерации commit message:

1. **Проверка Git репозитория**:  
   - Примеры команд для проверки наличия git репозитория:  
     - `git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree`  
     - `git status`  
   - Альтернативные методы проверки:  
     - Проверка наличия директории `.git`  
     - Использование `git rev-parse --git-dir`  
   - Пример реализации на Kotlin с обработкой ошибок:  
     ```kotlin
     fun isGitRepository(): Boolean {
         return try {
             val process = Runtime.getRuntime().exec("git rev-parse --is-inside-work-tree")
             process.waitFor() == 0
         } catch (e: Exception) {
             false
         }
     }
     ```

2. **Парсинг git status --porcelain**:  
   - Описание формата вывода команды `git status --porcelain`  
   - Статусные коды:  
     - `M` — модифицированный файл  
     - `A` — добавленный файл  
     - `D` — удалённый файл  
     - `R` — переименованный файл (формат: `R oldfile -> newfile`)  
     - `C` — скопированный файл  
     - `??` — неотслеживаемый файл  
   - Реализация функции `parseGitStatusPorcelain` на Kotlin с обработкой всех статусов, включая переименованные и неотслеживаемые файлы  
   - Примеры bash-скриптов для парсинга вывода:  
     ```bash
     git status --porcelain | while read line; do
         status=$(echo "$line" | cut -c1-2)
         file=$(echo "$line" | cut -c4-)
         echo "Status: $status, File: $file"
     done
     ```
   - Примеры вывода и ожидаемых результатов для различных сценариев

3. **Чтение данных задачи из tasks.json**:  
   - Реализация функции `readLocalTask` на Kotlin с поддержкой тегов  
   - Альтернативный метод прямого парсинга JSON с использованием библиотеки Jackson  
   - Примеры использования для извлечения id, title, description:  
     ```kotlin
     data class Task(val id: Int, val title: String, val description: String)
     fun readLocalTask(): Task {
         val json = File("tasks.json").readText()
         return jacksonObjectMapper().readValue(json, Task::class.java)
     }
     ```

4. **Дополнительные разделы**:  
   - **Command Reference**: Справочник всех команд workflow с примерами использования  
   - **Testing the Implementation**: Примеры тестирования парсинга и проверки git репозитория, включая сценарии с различными статусами файлов и ошибками

Вся документация содержит детальные примеры команд, реализации и тестирования для всех пяти шагов процесса генерации commit message.
</info added on 2025-11-30T17:18:42.913Z>

tasks.title: Configure jOOQ Code Generation Dependency for Kotlin Compilation and Validate Imports/Output Location
tasks.description: Ensure jOOQ classes are generated before Kotlin compilation and verify all jOOQ imports and output locations are correct.
tasks.details:
After recent changes, jOOQ-generated classes are not being generated automatically after a clean build, causing test compilation failures. To resolve this:

1. Update the Gradle build configuration to make the `compileKotlin` task depend on the `generateJooq` task. This ensures that jOOQ code generation always runs before Kotlin compilation.

   Example (Gradle Kotlin DSL):
   ```kotlin
   tasks.compileKotlin { dependsOn(tasks.named("generateJooq")) }
   ```

2. Verify that the jOOQ code generation configuration specifies the correct output directory (typically `build/generated-sources/jooq` or similar) and that this directory is included in the source sets for compilation.

3. Check all imports in test and main code that reference jOOQ-generated classes. Ensure they match the actual package and class names generated by jOOQ.

4. Confirm that the jOOQ configuration in `build.gradle.kts` or equivalent specifies the correct schema and target package, and that the generated classes appear in the expected location after running the build.

5. If using custom generator strategies or extensions, ensure they are correctly referenced and available on the classpath during code generation.

6. Run a clean build and verify that jOOQ classes are generated and available for compilation.

7. Ensure that tasks such as `compileKotlin`, `test`, and `build` are properly conditioned to run after the jOOQ code generation task, especially when using containerized databases for code generation.

8. Set up any additional tooling tasks (e.g., ktlint) to run after jOOQ code generation using `mustRunAfter` or similar mechanisms to avoid race conditions.
tasks.testStrategy:
1. Run a clean build (`./gradlew clean build`) and confirm that jOOQ-generated classes are present in the expected output directory.
2. Verify that all tests compile and run without errors related to missing jOOQ classes.
3. Check that all jOOQ imports in the codebase are correct and that generated classes are used as expected.
4. Confirm that the `compileKotlin` task does not start until after the `generateJooq` task has completed successfully.
5. Ensure that containerized database tasks are correctly conditioned to run before code generation and that all dependent tasks (compileKotlin, test, build) are executed after code generation.
6. Validate that tooling tasks like ktlint run after code generation and do not interfere with the build process.

tasks.title: Add Edge Case Tests for Validation, Pagination, and Coordinates
tasks.description: Implement comprehensive unit tests targeting boundary conditions for validation logic, pagination parameters, and coordinate inputs to ensure robust handling of edge cases.
tasks.details: Create two new Kotlin test classes: EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt and PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt. In EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt, develop tests covering boundary values for coordinate inputs, including NaN, Infinity, and out-of-range values, as well as string inputs with maximum length, minimum length, empty strings, and strings containing only whitespace. In PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt, implement tests for pagination parameters with edge cases such as negative numbers, extremely large values, and invalid string inputs. Use parameterized tests where appropriate to efficiently cover multiple edge scenarios. Ensure tests use the updated validation API and follow existing project conventions for assertions and test structure. Maintain test isolation and clarity with descriptive test names. Integrate these tests into the existing test suite to run automatically during CI builds.
tasks.testStrategy: Verify that all new tests in EdgeCaseValidationTest.kt and PaginationEdgeCaseTest.kt execute successfully without failures. Confirm that tests correctly detect invalid inputs by asserting expected validation errors or exceptions. Run the full test suite to ensure no regressions occur. Use parameterized test reports to confirm coverage of all specified edge cases. Review test logs to ensure descriptive output for each edge case scenario. Additionally, perform code review to ensure tests adhere to project standards and effectively cover boundary conditions as specified.

tasks.title: Analyze and Identify Duplicate and Redundant Tests
tasks.description: Perform a comprehensive analysis of all existing unit and integration test files to identify duplicate tests and redundant integration tests that replicate unit test logic.
tasks.details:
Scan test directories `src/test/kotlin/yayauheny/by/unit/` and `src/test/kotlin/yayauheny/by/integrationTest/` to catalog tests. Use static analysis tools or scripts to detect duplicate test scenarios and overlapping assertions. Mark integration tests that duplicate unit test coverage for removal, retaining only those covering end-to-end or cross-layer scenarios.
<info added on 2025-11-30T18:10:50.225Z>
Analysis phase completed: all 23 test files (17 unit, 6 integration) have been reviewed. Identified duplicates include RestroomApiTest duplicating RestroomControllerTest for GET /restrooms with pagination, GET /restrooms/{id}, and POST /restrooms (valid/invalid data), as well as CityApiTest and CountryApiTest duplicating corresponding unit tests for basic CRUD operations. The decision is to remove integration tests that duplicate controller/service logic and retain only integration tests covering end-to-end scenarios (e.g., GET /restrooms/nearest with real geo-queries), database-specific features (PostGIS, transactions), and cross-layer integration. A detailed report has been created at `.taskmaster/docs/test-analysis-report.md`. Next steps include starting the removal of duplicate tests and standardizing the test structure.
</info added on 2025-11-30T18:10:50.225Z>
tasks.testStrategy: Verify completeness by cross-referencing test coverage reports before and after deduplication. Ensure no critical business logic coverage is lost by running full test suite and comparing results.

tasks.title: Standardize Test Structure Using Given/When/Then Pattern
tasks.description: Refactor all tests to follow the Given/When/Then pattern with clear section comments to improve readability and maintainability.
tasks.details:
Update test methods to include explicit `// Given`, `// When`, and `// Then` comments. Ensure each test clearly separates setup, action, and assertion phases. Enforce this pattern in both unit and integration tests. Provide examples and guidelines for developers to follow.
<info added on 2025-11-30T18:12:42.896Z>
Progress update for task details:

Completed refactoring RestroomApiTest to fully adopt the Given/When/Then pattern with explicit `// Given`, `// When`, and `// Then` comments in all tests. Removed redundant CRUD tests covered by unit tests, retaining only end-to-end tests for the /nearest endpoint that involve real database queries. All tests now clearly separate setup, action, and assertion phases according to the pattern.

Next steps include continuing the standardization of integration test files such as CityApiTest and CountryApiTest, followed by applying the same Given/When/Then structure to unit test files. This phased approach ensures consistent test readability and maintainability across the codebase.
</info added on 2025-11-30T18:12:42.896Z>
